name: Operation Policy Review: Unraveling the Kimsuky Breach
description:
Scenario Overview

Our Security Operations Center (SOC) has detected a sequence of high-severity alerts originating from the workstation of a senior think-tank employee (User: klee). The incident appears to have started with a highly targeted spearphishing email carrying a suspicious attachment named DPRK_Policy_Review.chm.

The Threat

Based on initial triage, the attack bears the hallmarks of the Kimsuky APT group. Once the malicious CHM file was executed, it initiated a multi-stage infection chain that completely compromised the host. The adversary's activities include:

  • Initial Access & Execution: Leveraging hh.exe to spawn a command shell and drop a malicious VBScript (init.vbs).
  • Persistence & Evasion: Establishing a registry Run key and utilizing PowerShell to add Windows Defender exclusions.
  • Discovery & Collection: Harvesting system information, capturing active processes, stealing Chrome login data, and injecting a keylogger into explorer.exe.
  • Exfiltration: Compressing stolen data into a cabinet archive (temp.cab) using makecab.exe and exfiltrating it via HTTP POST to an external C2 server (27.102.114.89).

Your Objective

You have been provided with the raw JSON alert export containing both true positive malicious events and benign background noise (false positives). Your mission is to analyze these logs, accurately trace the attacker's footsteps along the kill chain, identify the indicators of compromise (IOCs), and reconstruct the entire attack narrative.

Do you have what it takes to dissect this APT intrusion? Happy hunting!
